title: How to reverse submental artery VO
filename: How to reverse submental artery VO.en.vtt
excerpt: We obviously don't be sticking needles into the carotid but you can only feel that so you can feel the bigger vessels how do we go deeper like where do we inject to get uh well that's a good question so what we agreed on the support group was that it might be better to use a cann…

segments.text: we obviously don't be sticking needles into the carotid but you can only feel that so you can feel the bigger vessels how do we go deeper like where do we inject to get uh well that's a good question so what we agreed on the support group was that it might be better to use a cannula where possible so you're just trying to decrease the amount of trauma but sliding a cannula underneath the jaw and into those delicate structures gently so you're getting the highlights there with the least amount of trauma seemed like a sensible course of action um but you know you need to know your neck anatomy to a degree as well we obviously don't be sticking needles into the carotid but you can only feel that so you can feel the bigger vessels and you just do low intervention like don't inject 100 times in one spot if you can help it cannula where possible probably larger doses if you know there's a compromise there get it in and then massage rather than doing repeated small doses these are just ideas in my mind about what might be better

but obviously it's a sensitive area so most clinicians intuitively know you're not just going to rush in and stab it 150 times and that case ended really well didn't it because the clinician just did very good was very comprehensive yeah so the clinician who was on our complications group um got a fantastic result there doesn't seem to be any tissue necrosis at all and it was a very large and very obvious and significant vascular occlusion so a lot complete power around the endpoint of the submental artery

transcript: we obviously don't be sticking needles into the carotid but you can only feel that so you can feel the bigger vessels how do we go deeper like where do we inject to get uh well that's a good question so what we agreed on the support group was that it might be better to use a cannula where possible so you're just trying to decrease the amount of trauma but sliding a cannula underneath the jaw and into those delicate structures gently so you're getting the highlights there with the least amount of trauma seemed like a sensible course of action um but you know you need to know your neck anatomy to a degree as well we obviously don't be sticking needles into the carotid but you can only feel that so you can feel the bigger vessels and you just do low intervention like don't inject 100 times in one spot if you can help it cannula where possible probably larger doses if you know there's a compromise there get it in and then massage rather than doing repeated small doses these are just ideas in my mind about what might be better but obviously it's a sensitive area so most clinicians intuitively know you're not just going to rush in and stab it 150 times and that case ended really well didn't it because the clinician just did very good was very comprehensive yeah so the clinician who was on our complications group um got a fantastic result there doesn't seem to be any tissue necrosis at all and it was a very large and very obvious and significant vascular occlusion so a lot complete power around the endpoint of the submental artery
